reasoning: Applied CEFR companion volume (2020) conversation descriptors across five dimensions: range, accuracy, fluency, interaction, coherence. Pablo demonstrates solid B2 competence with C1 flashes in range and interaction, but fossilized L1 transfer errors in accuracy (null subjects, false friends, prepositional calques) prevent classification as B2+ or C1. The error profile is characteristic of an upper B2 Spanish-English speaker: strong communicative competence undermined by persistent structural transfer. Assessment confidence is high due to the 60-minute sample covering multiple communicative functions (narrating, arguing, negotiating, formal writing) across professional and personal topics.
suggestions: Design targeted practice for null subject insertion in embedded clauses. Use gap-fill exercises with "I think ___ is", "The problem is that ___ is" structures. This is the highest-priority fossilized error.
Create a false friends flashcard set for the 4 pairs identified (assist/attend, sensible/sensitive, comprehensive/understanding, actually/currently). Review at start of each lesson.
Collocation drills using make/do/give/take/hold/attend with business nouns. Use matching and categorization exercises.
Preposition focus: create sentences requiring "depend on", "interested in", "consist of", "independent of", "discuss [direct object]". Drill until automatic.
Continue debate-format activities - they generate rich output and surface errors under communicative pressure. Introduce more hedging and academic register.
Start the false friends journal as discussed. Add a sentence pair for each: "comprensivo = understanding, comprehensive = complete".
Before sending work emails, check for: missing "it" subjects, false friends (actually/currently, assist/attend), and collocations (make/do/give).
Record the 3-minute AI argument as homework. Listen back and count null subject omissions.
Read one Economist article daily. Underline collocations - especially make/do/give/take patterns.
Switch Freakonomics podcast to English version for commute listening.
